description: Context fitness-function eval system: deterministic Tier-A probes (exit-code oracle, real-state) registered per lesson, a shared ablation gate generalizing /context-audit Tier-2's restore mechanics, an evals/RESULTS.md benchmark, a /eval runner skill + weekly cron, and a harden/proceduralize/eval triage. Tier-B behavioral evals are out of scope for v1.

userStories.title: Establish evals/ corpus and probe contract
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need a defined home and convention for probes so every later story has a contract to target.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/ directory created with a tracked README.md per context/rules/directory-readme.md (intent, subfolder table, conventions, pointer to this PRD)
Probe convention documented: a probe is evals/probes/<id>.sh; 3-state exit oracle is 0=PASS, 1=REGRESSION, 2=SKIPPED/not-applicable; stderr carries a one-line reason
Probe header convention: comment lines '# tier: A|ablation', '# source: <ref>', '# desc: <one line>'; exact extraction contract is grep -E '^# (tier|source|desc):'

evals/RESULTS.md schema defined (columns: probe id | tier | last-run UTC | status | source); policy is OVERWRITE the current-status row per probe-id, git history is the time series (no unbounded append) [M-4]
.gitignore reviewed: probe scripts and RESULTS.md are tracked; ablation temp copies are not
shellcheck passes on any example probe shipped
userStories.notes: Done 2026-06-10 (Wave 1): evals/README.md (contract: 3-state oracle, header grep, triage), evals/RESULTS.md (overwrite-row schema).

userStories.title: /eval runner skill
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want one command that runs the probe suite against real state and records results so the benchmark is a single invocation.
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/eval/SKILL.md created; discovers evals/probes/*.sh, executes each wrapped in timeout (default 30s; hung probe marked TIMEOUT, non-PASS) [M-6]
Collects the 3-state exit code (0/1/2) + stderr reason; renders SKIPPED distinctly from PASS in RESULTS.md and never counts SKIPPED as PASS or REGRESSION [M-5]

Writes/updates evals/RESULTS.md with per-probe status and an explicit delta vs previous run; on FIRST run (no prior file) every probe is new-pass/new-fail and NO REGRESSION is raised without prior state [M-4]
A green->red transition is reported as a REGRESSION line naming the probe's source, at the top of the run summary
On startup, detects orphaned *.bak from a crashed prior ablation and restores them before running [M-2]
Supports --probe <id> and --tier A filters
Appends a Memory Improvement Protocol log entry per context/rules/memory.md
Adds bare entries eval, health-check, retro under 'Orchestrator skills' in .claude/protected-paths.txt in this same change [M-3]
Registered in the orchestrator skills table in AGENTS.md (edit the symlink target via readlink -f CLAUDE.md, not a resolved copy)
shellcheck / markdown structure check passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 contract. Folds M-2/M-3/M-4/M-5/M-6.

userStories.title: Seed probe next-dev-prod (Tier-A state probe)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want the recurring next dev leak encoded as a probe so the omission can never be silently re-paid.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/probes/next-dev-prod.sh inspects real state (running processes / tmux pane serving public mifunedev/website) and exits 1 (REGRESSION) while the public site is served by next dev
When the website sandbox is absent it exits 2 (SKIPPED) with a clear stderr reason; exit 0 on an absent sandbox is FORBIDDEN [M-5]
Completes within the default probe timeout; no hang on an unreachable sandbox [M-6]
Header declares '# tier: A', '# source: memory/MEMORY.md 2026-06-04'
Running /eval --probe next-dev-prod records the probe in RESULTS.md (expected current status: REGRESSION)
shellcheck passes
userStories.notes: Catches the omission failure that motivated the system.

userStories.title: Harden the /dev/tcp-in-zsh lesson (hook + unit-test probe)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want the /dev/tcp foot-gun warned-on deterministically rather than relied on as a remembered rule.
acceptanceCriteria: A PreToolUse Bash hook under .claude/hooks/ detects /dev/tcp/ in a command and emits a NON-BLOCKING warning (stderr, exit 0) recommending ss/curl/nc; it must not deny the command [M-7]
Hook registered in .claude/settings.json under hooks.PreToolUse following the existing deny-env-dump.sh pattern (bash "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"/.claude/hooks/<script>) [M-7]
No false-positive on substrings like .devcontainer or paths merely containing dev (explicit command-position class, not \b)
evals/probes/devtcp-hook.sh unit-tests the hook via the file-fixture driver pattern (tokens in shell vars, driver written to a file) and asserts the warning fires; exit 0=PASS
Header declares '# tier: A', '# source: memory/MEMORY.md 2026-06-10 (zsh /dev/tcp)'
shellcheck passes on hook + probe
userStories.notes: Harden surface (not a behavioral eval). settings.json wiring confirmed to exist.

userStories.title: Proceduralize docker stats into /health-check (step + doc-lint probe)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want the diagnose-RAM-with-docker-stats technique baked into the skill, guarded by a probe that the step still exists.
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/health-check/SKILL.md gains an explicit step: when memory is the binding constraint, run docker stats per-container and drop into the heaviest container's process list — EXTENDS, does not remove any existing ladder step [FR-10]
evals/probes/health-check-docker-stats.sh doc-lints the skill: greps for the docker stats step and exits 1 if absent, 0 if present
Header declares '# tier: A', '# source: memory/MEMORY.md 2026-06-10 (docker stats vs ps Size)'
shellcheck passes; /health-check skill structure intact
userStories.notes: Proceduralize surface. The doc-lint probe is the regression guard for the added step.

userStories.title: Ablation gate via shared scripts/ablate.sh
userStories.description: As a context curator, I want to run a probe with and without a target context file so I can prove the file load-bearing before deleting it.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ablate.sh extracts ONLY the file swap/restore/trap mechanics from /context-audit SKILL.md:255-268 (cp .bak -> trap mv EXIT -> swap -> restore); both /eval and /context-audit source it — no duplication, and the claude -p oracle is NOT reused [M-1]

/eval --ablate <context-file> --probe <id> runs the probe with the target file present and removed; the oracle is the shell probe's exit code; reports LOAD-BEARING (regression on removal) or PRUNABLE (no change) [M-1]
git diff --exit-code HEAD passes after each run; ablate.sh writes the .bak path to stderr at start so /eval can recover orphans after a hard kill [M-2]
/context-audit existing Tier-2 behavior is unchanged (it now sources the shared helper instead of inlining it)
Result recorded distinctly in RESULTS.md (tier 'ablation')
shellcheck / markdown check passes
userStories.notes: Prune-half of the fitness function. Reuse boundary corrected per §10 M-1 (mechanics not oracle).

userStories.title: Weekly cron: scheduled benchmark + regression surfacing
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want the suite to run unattended weekly so regressions surface without a human remembering to run /eval.
acceptanceCriteria: Implementer FIRST reads scripts/cron-runtime.ts to confirm how a cron task runs a skill; if skill-delegation is unsupported, crons/eval-weekly.md INLINES probe discovery+run rather than delegating to /eval [M-8]
crons/eval-weekly.md created following the existing cron spec shape (compatible with scripts/cron-runtime.ts), overlap: false
On any REGRESSION the cron writes a dated entry to memory/<UTC-date>/log.md naming the regressed probe and its source; log-only is accepted v1 behavior (auto-issue/notification is v2) [M-8]
Does not modify or remove crons/heartbeat.md or crons/cleanup-tasks.md [FR-10]
A crons/README.md row documents the new entry
shellcheck / markdown check passes
userStories.notes: Depends on /eval. Cron executability gated on reading cron-runtime.ts first.

userStories.title: /retro integration — minimal triage tag (v1)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator closing a session, I want /retro to route each supported lesson to its cheapest correction surface so lessons become closeable instead of becoming bullets.
acceptanceCriteria: v1 ships ONLY the minimal triage tag: .claude/skills/retro/SKILL.md tags each supported promotable lesson with harden|proceduralize|eval and proposes a probe id (or hook/skill-step) [M-9]
The triage defaults away from behavioral evals; proposing a Tier-B eval requires an explicit note
/retro existing scientific loop AND propose-then-confirm gate are PRESERVED, not replaced; recurrence-escalation + deeper integration are explicitly split to a follow-up [M-9]
A follow-up note is recorded in the PRD/issue for the split-out recurrence-escalation work
Markdown structure check passes
userStories.notes: Scope split per §10 M-9 to protect the confirm gate.
